name: roundtrip_external_body_trusted
reason: `theorem_serialize_parse_roundtrip` is `external_body` with `unimplemented!()` — the fundamental serialize-parse roundtrip correctness is entirely trusted without verification

name: parse_productive_external_body_trusted
code:
proof fn phi_2_parse_productive_external_body_trusted<C: SecureSpecCombinator>(
    c: &C,
    s: Seq<u8>,
    n: int,
    v: C::Type,
)
    requires
        c.requires(),
        c.is_productive(),
        c.spec_parse(s) == Some((n, v)),
    ensures
        n > 0,
{
    c.lemma_parse_productive(s);
}
reason: `lemma_parse_productive` is `external_body` with `unimplemented!()` — the productivity guarantee (parse consumes >0 bytes) is trusted without proof, allowing a non-productive parser to be accepted as productive

name: serialize_productive_from_two_externals
reason: Serialize productivity chains two `external_body` axioms (roundtrip + parse_productive) — if either trusted axiom is wrong, a zero-length serialization would be accepted for a supposedly productive combinator

name: wf_default_true_vacuous
reason: The default `wf` returns `true` for all values — any combinator that forgets to override `wf` has the roundtrip and productivity theorems' preconditions trivially satisfied for ALL values including malformed ones
